title: Local Authorities Superannuation Fund (Pension Management) Rules, 2022
citation: Local Authorities Superannuation Fund (Pension Management) Rules, 2022

sections.heading: These Rules may be cited as the Local Authorities Title
sections.text:
These Rules may be cited as the Local Authorities Title
Superannuation Fund (Pension Management) Rules, 2022.

sections.heading: In these Rules, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
sections.text:
In these Rules, unless the context otherwise requires— Interpretation
“associated authority” has the meaning assigned to the words
in the Act;
“beneficiary” means a person receiving a benefit other than
a member;
“Board” means a Board established under section 5 of the
Act;
“contribution statement” means a member’s record of
contributions;
“dependant” has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Act;
“eligible employee” has the meaning assigned to the words in
the Act;
“inactive member” means
(a) a member who is in receipt of an annuity, but whose
record has been dormant for a period of five years
or more; or
(b) a contributing member whose contributions have
not been received for a continuous period of twelve
months;
Copies of this Statutory Instrument can be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O.Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka Price K52.00 each
34 Statutory Instruments 25th February, 2022
“member” has the meaning assigned to the word in the Act;
“personal representative” has the meaning assigned to the
Cap. 60 words in the Wills and Administration of Testate Estate
Act; and
“public body” has the meaning assigned to the words in the
Act No. 1 of Public Finance Management Act, 2018.
2018
PART II
REGISTRATION
Registration 3. (1) A local authority or public body shall apply to the Board
of local for registration in Form I set out in the Schedule.
authority
or public (2) The Board may, within sixty days of receipt of an application
body
under sub rule (1), grant or reject the application.
(3) The Board shall, where the Board rejects an application
under sub rule (2), inform the applicant, in writing, and give reasons
for the rejection.
(4) The Board shall, where the Board grants an application under
sub rule (2), inform the applicant in writing.
(5) The Board shall, within fourteen days of granting the
application, register a local authority or public body as an associated
authority of the Fund and assign the associated authority an—
(a) effective date of registration; and
(b) employer number for the purposes of the Fund.
Registration 4. (1) An associated authority shall register an eligible
of employee with the Fund as a member within ninety days of being
eligible
employee admitted as an associated authority in Form II set out in the Schedule.
(2) Subject to sub rule (1), an associated authority shall register
a subsequent eligible employee with the Fund as a member within
thirty days of the date of appointment of the eligible employee.
(3) The Fund shall, within fourteen days of registration of an
eligible employee as a member in accordance with sub-rules (1)
and (2), assign the member
(a) an effective date of registration; and
(b) a social security number for the purposes of the Fund.
25th February, 2022 Statutory Instruments 35
PART III
CONTRIBUTIONS

sections.heading: An associated authority shall submit contribution schedules Contribution
sections.text:
An associated authority shall submit contribution schedules Contribution
schedules
to the Board on the 7th day of the month following the month
when emoluments are earned by a member as set out in Form III
of the Schedule.

sections.heading: An associated authority and the Board shall conduct an Reconciliation
sections.text:
An associated authority and the Board shall conduct an Reconciliation
annual reconciliation of contributions before the end of the first of
contribution
quarter of the following year for purposes of ensuring compliance
with the Act and these Rules.

sections.heading: (1) The Board may, on its own motion, refund to a member Contributions
sections.text:
(1) The Board may, on its own motion, refund to a member Contributions
contributions made in error to the Fund. made in error
(2) A member or personal representative of a deceased member
may apply to the Board for a refund of a contribution made in
error in Form IV set out in the Schedule.
(3) The Board may, within thirty days of receipt of an application
under sub rule (2), grant or reject the application.
(4) The Board shall, where it rejects an application under sub-
rule (3), inform the applicant in writing, and give reasons for the
rejection.
(5) The Board shall, where it grants an application under sub-
rule (3), inform the applicant, in writing, and remit the refund to the
member or personal representative.
(6) Despite sub-rule (5), the Board may retain the whole or
part of the amount to be refunded to a member or personal
representative eligible for a refund and apply it to an outstanding
debt.

sections.heading: (1) Where a member is seconded by an associated authority Contributions
sections.text:
(1) Where a member is seconded by an associated authority Contributions
to an institution that is not an associated authority, the period that on
secondment
the member is seconded shall not be reckoned when calculating
the continuous service of the member under the Act.
(2) A member who wishes to have the period referred to under
sub rule (1) reckoned when calculating the continuous service, shall
apply to the Board to continue making contributions based on the
last salary received prior to the secondment in Form V set out in
the Schedule.
(3) The Board shall, within thirty days of receipt of an application
under sub rule (2), grant or reject the application.
(4) The Board shall, where it rejects an application under sub-
rule (3), inform the applicant, in writing, and give reasons for the
rejection.
36 Statutory Instruments 25th February, 2022
(5) The Board shall, where it grants the application under sub-
rule (3), inform the applicant, in writing, and that the applicant shall
continue to remit contributions accordingly.
(6) The contributions referred to in this rule shall cover both
the amount payable by the member and the associated authority.
Failure to pay 9. An associated authority that fails to pay a contribution or
or remit remit a contribution of a member due to the Fund commits an offence
contribution
and is liable, on conviction, to a fine not exceeding two thousand
five hundred penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not
exceeding two years, or to both.
PART IV
MEMBER’S RECORD
Contribution 10. The Board shall furnish every member with a contribution
statements statement on an annual basis.
Maintenance 11. (1) The Board shall cause to be kept proper records of
of members who are
records
(a) currently contributing to the Board;
(b) in receipt of a lump sum;
(c) in receipt of an annuity; and
(d) in receipt of a refund.
(2) The records referred to under sub-rule (1) shall be kept in
the custody of the Managing Director at the registered offices of
the Fund, shall be open to that member for inspection during normal
office hours.
Confidentiality 12. (1) A member’s record or any other information relating to
of member’s a member under the Fund is confidential.
records
(2) Despite sub rule (1)—
(a) a member shall have the right to access that member’s
record maintained by the Board;
(b) a personal representative of a deceased member shall
have the right to request and access information relating
to the deceased member’s record; or
(c) an associated authority may request for information
relating to a member as determined by the Board.
(3) A request for information relating to a member under sub-
rule (2) (b) and (c) shall be made in writing and signed by the
personal representative, or a chief executive officer or the head of
a human resource management department of an associated
authority
25th February, 2022 Statutory Instruments 37

sections.heading: (1) A member who on inspection of a member’s record, Amendment
sections.text:
(1) A member who on inspection of a member’s record, Amendment
of
intends to amend an erroneous entry on the record with the Board,
records
shall apply to the Board in Form VI set out in the Schedule.
(2) The Board shall, within thirty days of receipt of an application
under sub rule (1), grant or reject the application.
(3) The Board shall, where it —
(a) rejects the application, inform the applicant, in writing
and give reasons for the rejection; or
(b) grants the application, inform the applicant , in writing
and amend the member’s record accordingly.

sections.heading: The Board shall archive a member’s record who is over Archiving of
sections.text:
The Board shall archive a member’s record who is over Archiving of
member’s
the age of hundred years and who has not claimed their annuity in
record
the last one hundred and twenty months or more.

sections.heading: (1) The Board shall maintain registers of members and Registers of
sections.text:
(1) The Board shall maintain registers of members and Registers of
members
associated authorities, in a manner determined by the Board.
(2) The Board shall enter in the registers referred to under sub-
rule (1), the details and particulars relating to
(a) associated authorities registered with the Fund;
(b) active members;
(c) inactive members; and
(d) any other information that the Board may determine.
(3) The registers referred to under sub-rule (1), shall be kept in
the custody of the Managing Director at the registered offices of
the Fund, and shall be open for inspection by members during office
hours on payment of a fee as the Board may determine.
(4) The Managing Director shall, on the direction of the Board,
cause copies of a register and any alterations of, or additions to,
the register to be printed and published in a manner and form that
the Board may determine.
(5) Subject to these Rules, a copy of the last published and
printed register shall be prima facie evidence of what is contained
in the register and the absence of the name of a member or
associated authority shall be prima facie evidence that the member
or associated authority is not registered.
38 Statutory Instruments 25th February, 2022
PART V
BENEFITS
Accrual of 16. (1) A retirement benefit shall start accruing on a member’s
retirement last working day.
benefit
(2) A member’s retirement benefit shall cease to accrue where
a member is over the age of hundred years and has not made a
claim for an annuity in the last one hundred and twenty months or
more.
Adjustment 17. (1) Subject to the Constitution, the Board may adjust the
of minimum benefit to be paid to a member that the Board may
benefit
Cap.1 determine.
(2) An adjustment of the minimum benefit is valid if the
adjustment is supported by financial performance of the Fund and
actuarial advice from an actuary.

sections.heading: (1) A member, dependant or personal representative shall
sections.text:
(1) A member, dependant or personal representative shall
Submission of
claims submit a claim for a benefit to the Board in Form VII(a) set out in
the Schedule.
(2) A claim made under sub-rule (1) shall be—
(a) certified by an associated authority in Form VII (a) set
out in the Schedule; and
(b) accompanied by bank details in Form VII (b) set out in
the Schedule.
(3) The Board shall return a claim referred to in sub-rule (1) to
a member, dependant or personal representative if the claim is
incomplete and request the member, dependant or personal
representative to complete the form.
(4) The Board shall, on receipt of a complete claim made under
this rule, assess the claim and pay a benefit to a member or
beneficiary.
Determination 19. The Board shall, where a member, dependant or personal
of date of representative submits a national registration card for a member
birth
that only indicates the year of birth of that member deem the date
of birth of the member as the 1st of July of the year indicated on
the national registration card for the purposes of recording and
computing the member’s retirement benefit.
Submission 20. (1) A member below the age of seventy-five shall submit
of life to the Board a Life Certificate every two years in Form VIII set
certificate by
member out in the Schedule.
25th February, 2022 Statutory Instruments 39
(2) A member aged seventy-five and above shall submit to the
Board a Life Certificate every year in Form VIII set out in the
Schedule.
(3) A benefit shall not be paid where a member has not
submitted a Life Certificate in accordance with sub rules (1) or
(2).

sections.heading: A widow receiving an annuity shall submit to the Board an Affirmation
sections.text:
A widow receiving an annuity shall submit to the Board an Affirmation
affirmation of widow status in Form IX set out in the Schedule to of widow
status
enable the Board assess the widow’s continued eligibility to receive
an annuity.
PART VI
GENERAL PROVISIONS

sections.heading: Subject to section 46 of the Act, a member or an associated Change of
sections.text:
Subject to section 46 of the Act, a member or an associated Change of
authority shall notify the Managing Director in writing, of a change particulars
in the particulars of that member or associated authority for the
purposes of the Fund, within fourteen days of that change.

sections.heading: (1) A person shall not, without the consent, in writing, given Prohibition of
sections.text:
(1) A person shall not, without the consent, in writing, given Prohibition of
publication or
by or on behalf of the Board, publish or disclose to any person,
disclosure of
otherwise than in the course of that person’s duties, the contents of information
to
a document, communication or information which relates to, and
unauthorised
which comes to that person’s knowledge in the course of that persons
person’s duties under the Act and these Rules.
(2) A person who contravenes sub rule (1) commits an offence
and is liable, on conviction, to fine not exceeding two thousand five
hundred penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding
two years, or to both.
(3) A person who, having information which to the knowledge
of that person has been published or disclosed in contravention of
sub rule (1), unlawfully publishes or communicates the information
to another person, commits an offence and is liable, on conviction,
to a fine not exceeding two thousand five hundred penalty units or
for imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years, or to both.

sections.heading: (1) The Board shall suspend payment of a benefit where— Suspension
sections.text:
(1) The Board shall suspend payment of a benefit where— Suspension
of
(a) a member fails to submit a Life Certificate in accordance benefit
payment
with rule 20; or
(b) a widow fails to submit an affirmation of widow status in
accordance with rule 21.
40 Statutory Instruments 25th February, 2022
(2) The Board may, before suspending the payment of a benefit
under sub rule (1), notify the member or widow of the Board’s
intention to suspend payment of benefit and shall—
(a) give reasons for the intended suspension; and
(b) require the member or widow to
(i)show cause, within a reasonable period that the
Board may specify in the notice, why the
payment of a benefit should not be suspended;
or
(ii) remedy the default.
(3) The Fund shall not suspend the payment of a benefit if the
member or widow takes remedial measures to the satisfaction of
the Fund within the period specified under sub rule (2).
(4) The suspension under this rule shall be lifted where a member
or widow takes the necessary steps required to enable the Board
to resume payment of a benefit in accordance with the Act or
these rules.
(5) The Board shall, where it suspends payment of a benefit,
publish the suspension, in a daily newspaper of general circulation
in the Republic.
Appeals 25. A person aggrieved with the decision of the Board under
these Rules may appeal to the Minister.
25th February, 2022 Statutory Instruments 41

sections.heading:  A certified copy of the evidence of death, order of appointment of Administrator,
sections.text:
 A certified copy of the evidence of death, order of appointment of Administrator,
Marriage certificate/Affidavit of marriage, Administrator’s National Registration Card
should be attached for all death cases.
 Letter of notice from the employer must be attached in all applications under section

sections.heading:  All applications must be supported by a covering letter from the employer.
sections.text:
 All applications must be supported by a covering letter from the employer.
 All applications must be supported by two recent passport size photos for the member
and for the appointed Administrator for cases under section 35
 A certified copy of the last pay statement should be attached in all cases
 A covering letter from the employer must be attached in all cases
 LASF form VIII(b) must be attached for all cases
